context: Tests whether the agent respects service object boundary rules: keeping the orchestrator's call method concise by extracting sub-services, ensuring services return data (not HTTP responses), and avoiding cross-domain model modifications.

checklist.name: Orchestrator call length
checklist.description: The main orchestrator service's `call` (or `def call`) instance method body is 20 lines or fewer (comments and blank lines excluded)

checklist.name: Sub-services extracted
checklist.description: At least two distinct sub-service classes or helper classes are created to handle steps that the orchestrator delegates to

checklist.name: Orchestrator delegates, not implements
checklist.description: The orchestrator's `call` method calls other service objects or methods rather than containing the full implementation logic inline

checklist.name: No HTTP response returned
checklist.description: No service class renders or returns an HTTP response object (no `render`, `redirect_to`, `ActionDispatch::Response`, or `head` calls inside any service)

checklist.name: Data-only return
checklist.description: All services return plain Ruby hashes or data objects, not controller/HTTP concerns

checklist.name: Single responsibility
checklist.description: Each service class addresses one domain concern and does NOT modify models clearly outside its declared scope

checklist.name: Frozen string literal
checklist.description: Every service file begins with `# frozen_string_literal: true`

checklist.name: File structure
checklist.description: All service files are placed under `app/services/<module>/` with namespaced module declarations

checklist.name: Response format
checklist.description: Return values across all services use `{ success: true/false, response: { ... } }` structure

checklist.name: Error handling
checklist.description: At least one service has a rescue block that returns an error hash rather than leaking exceptions
